name: ux-psychology
description: 21 evidence-based UX psychology principles for building interfaces. Persuasion: smart defaults, goal gradient, reciprocity, IKEA effect, loss aversion, anchoring. Interaction laws: Fitts, Hick, Jakob, Miller, Tesler, Doherty, Postel, Pareto. Perception: Gestalt, Von Restorff, serial position, peak-end, Zeigarnik, aesthetic-usability, banner blindness. Plus Nielsen's 10 heuristics, perceived performance (skeletons, optimistic UI, labor illusion), form microcopy, trust patterns, and a review checklist. Every principle includes guardrails separating honest persuasion from dark patterns.
